message: The new contract-review precision fields are parsed into contractReviewFinding but are not persisted to contract/reviewer/findings.jsonl.
evidence: writeContractReviewFindingsJSONL converts each parsed finding into contractReviewFindingLine, whose persisted fields omit material_impact, fix_direction, uncertainty, and state.

message: The durable contract-review findings artifact still drops material_impact, fix_direction, uncertainty, and state.
evidence: writeContractReviewFindingsJSONL converts each contractReviewFinding into contractReviewFindingLine with only id, fingerprint, category, message, and blocking. contractReviewFindingLine has no material_impact, fix_direction, uncertainty, or state fields, so values parsed into f are not persisted to contract/reviewer/findings.jsonl despite AC1 requiring serialization and persistence.

message: New-field persistence is not tested; the JSONL artifact test only checks legacy fields, so material_impact, fix_direction, uncertainty, and state can be dropped without failing the suite.
evidence: TestContractReviewFindingsJSONLWrittenAtLoopEnd reads findings.jsonl but only asserts message/category/blocking. The writer still builds contractReviewFindingLine with only id, fingerprint, category, message, and blocking at internal/app/contract_review.go:977, so the acceptance-required new fields are not covered by persistence tests.

message: The contract reviewer prompt rule text is not covered by a discriminating test.
evidence: TestContractReviewerPromptContainsContractSchema asserts the contract schema and absence of the code-review schema only. There are no assertions for material_impact, fix_direction, uncertainty, state documentation, the HARD RULE, or the advisory wording/completeness preference rule, despite the contract requiring prompt-rule test coverage.
